author: @TheAndreasMustola
text: A suggestion for a future video, on how to use the parts from a pc screen, is to use the acrylic for a light window, or fake indoor window.

author: @KaliFissure
text:
Tesla turbine to drive a boat. Multiple large flat disks with small but some separation between (experiment to optimize) spinning like wheels at side of craft. The surface tension clings to disks but will self drain. 
I'm pretty sure you could get both crazy efficiency and speed. As soon as I have a house again I'm working on pedal powered kayak using this.  

Like your more recent video (which has comments off?) only multiple disks and the disks move to drive craft.

author: @docink6175
text: I use a jet of water from a hose to clean fans and paint rollers. Kind of like the magnus effect cylinders used  as turbines and even sails.. If you hang a paint roller in a 5gallon bucket and aim a jet of water at it it will spin at fantastic speeds, you can get different speeds/sounds by using different nap roller, a thin nap gives a higher note than a thick one. following this idea you should be able to generate electric power., you just need a steady water source... an air stream will also make them spin and dry but again you need a steady supply of air...  You can also create splatter art by blowing a jet of air over a paint laden roller but thats a different subject altogether ....

author: @AdricM
text: looks like your water float berring could use a slimmer bean tin,  or some pipe or something inside to cause a lot less lateral slop,  still good talk/info

author: @yasararif8292
text:
How about the pressure from gas pipes that is servicing homes?

Can that gas still be used to fuel homes and as a bi product produce electricity?

replies.author: @samuelmendoza9356
replies.text: Maybe a combination of heat and energy. Gas heats the central heater and the waste gas goes to the Stirling. Or the gas heats the Stirling engine and the heat goes to the central heater.

author: @Alex-tj1zo
text:
... wich brings up another question:
-Can we convert to electricity the tap water pressure ? 
I'm wery interested in your opinion on this.
I'm thinking about it for some time now and not only as a proof of concept, but a real, functional and efficient way to trap that wasted energy every time you open the foset.

replies.author: @TimeSurfer206
replies.text:
Well... If you're thinking of using Municipal Water to power the house, don't bother. It'd cost you more in water than the electricity is worth.
And even just as a scavenger from when you're flushing, showering, etc.? The amount of electricity scavenged would be miniscule compared to the cost of the gear.
Now, if you're scavenging electricity through Regenerative Braking on a 1,500 kg car slowing from 120 Km/H, is totally worth.

replies.author: @dismayedtrinket2518
replies.text:
@TimeSurfer206 - when I lived in an apartment I payed for electricity, but the water was free.

I was unhappy enough with the management that I was daydreaming about such.

replies.author: @TimeSurfer206
replies.text: @dismayedtrinket2518 I can totally sympathize, being in a very similar boat atm.

author: @timmcgirl5588
text: graphene circuits can harvest limitless zero emission energy called pedesis at room temperature and at normal atmospheric pressure.

replies.author: @Earzone63
replies.text: but what's the real limitation? you probably need kilometers^2 of it to be useable.. air source heat pumps are more practical, though noisy. hmm

author: @infinitelyuniversal2390
text:

You make a circle you have wheel, ad some grooves and you have a gear, add fins and you have turbine/prop.  
An intelligent shape design that does nothing on its own, yet harvests its environmental elemental forces to transform energy flow, redirecting all from the single force called intention, the reasoning behind the intelligent design since before when it was just an idea 💡 and then comes to life.  I find Nature so does the same but nature is very patient 😌 and makes the most beautiful and creative adaptations of life including us as humans see and create continuously. 
When we do anything we must ask are selves why? The drive to get to our unknown destination *

author: @totherarf
text:
One thing that never ceases to amaze me is Energy!
More specifically Energy Density!

If you have a hand cranked generator it can really show exactly how much grunt it takes to put any kind of useful charge into anything! It is only by thinking in terms of how much grunt you are willing to put into something compared with how much grunt you can harvest for free that you gain appreciation of exactly how good stuff is getting.

Often, because the source of grunt is free we tend to disparage the whole thing, after all we can go and buy a petrol generator that will out perform any of our "toys" ...... but we are not comparing like with like! 

Grunt scavenging is a thing and will continue to be more of a thing as time goes on.
Let celebrate those grunt scavengers for what they are ..... Heroes of innovation!
